summary.name: Quarterly US REIT Momentum Rotation Strategy
summary.author: Gurumeher Sawhney
summary.description: The strategy is a cross sectional momentum rotation within the US REIT universe, premised on the empirical persistence of intermediate term winners. summary.description_full:
The strategy is a cross sectional momentum rotation within the US REIT universe, premised on the empirical persistence of intermediate term winners. Each quarter it ranks liquid REITs by prior 11 month total return using a 1 month skip (i.e., performance from 12 months ago to 1 month ago) to reduce short term mean reversion and microstructure effects, then retains only the top third of the momentum distribution as the investable set. The opportunity targeted is sustained relative strength within the REIT sector, harvested by periodically reallocating toward the strongest names while avoiding the weakest.

The investable universe is defined from daily fundamental and price data as securities classified as REITs with fundamental coverage, price above 1 USD, and minimum average daily volume above 10,000 shares. Portfolio decisions are made on a quarterly schedule (month starts of January, April, July, October), with positions set to equal weight across the selected REITs and implicitly fully invested, reconstituting the portfolio at each rebalance. Risk controls are limited to liquidity and price filters plus diversification via equal weighting; there are no explicit volatility targeting, drawdown limits, or stop loss rules. A 100 day warm up is used to allow immediate initial positioning.

raw.name: Quarterly US REIT Momentum Rotation Strategy

author.name: Gurumeher Sawhney
author.bio: Previously Quantitative Support and Developer @ QuantConnect

raw.description:
The strategy is a cross sectional momentum rotation within the US REIT universe, premised on the empirical persistence of intermediate term winners. Each quarter it ranks liquid REITs by prior 11 month total return using a 1 month skip (i.e., performance from 12 months ago to 1 month ago) to reduce short term mean reversion and microstructure effects, then retains only the top third of the momentum distribution as the investable set. The opportunity targeted is sustained relative strength within the REIT sector, harvested by periodically reallocating toward the strongest names while avoiding the weakest.

The investable universe is defined from daily fundamental and price data as securities classified as REITs with fundamental coverage, price above 1 USD, and minimum average daily volume above 10,000 shares. Portfolio decisions are made on a quarterly schedule (month starts of January, April, July, October), with positions set to equal weight across the selected REITs and implicitly fully invested, reconstituting the portfolio at each rebalance. Risk controls are limited to liquidity and price filters plus diversification via equal weighting; there are no explicit volatility targeting, drawdown limits, or stop loss rules. A 100 day warm up is used to allow immediate initial positioning.
